domain: Sundanese angklung octave-pair shaken bamboo (each unit = 2 bamboo tubes in octave pair sounded together by single shake; one-player-one-pitch hocketed ensemble; ensemble distributes melody across players each owning one pitch class)
specific_mechanism: A two-tube octave-paired oscillator always fires simultaneously when its player shakes the unit; the pair always co-activates with octave-doubled spectrum; ensemble routes melody by hocketing single-pitch pair-owners.
llm_application: ANGKLUNG-OCTAVE 2-shard paired expert routing in sparse MoE: group N experts into N/2 PAIRS (E_a^(i), E_b^(i)); router routes a token to a PAIR (top-k at pair level), and the pair always FIRES BOTH members simultaneously with HALF each individual expert's hidden dim. Within a pair, FFN weights are tied as W_b = O W_a where O is a FIXED sparse 'octave permutation' operator (mapping low-band encoding to high-band). The pair produces a structured 2-octave output naturally; the model never activates a single expert in isolation. Differs from (a) GShard top-k expert (single expert routing), (b) DeepSeek shared expert (singleton not pair), (c) MoHGE grouped experts (group routing without octave tied weights), (d) parameter sharing PEFT (cross-layer not in-pair MoE) by combining (i) PAIR-LEVEL top-k routing + (ii) FIXED OCTAVE-OPERATOR weight tie within pair + (iii) HALF capacity per member.
novelty_claim: GShard MoE routes top-k expert. DeepSeek MoE adds shared expert. MoHGE groups experts with two-level routing. PEFT cross-layer tie. ANGKLUNG-OCTAVE recombines (a) PAIR-LEVEL routing + (b) FIXED OCTAVE OPERATOR tie + (c) HALF-CAPACITY pair members. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
